name: Greenie two-on-one-hole — proves the COUNT model end-to-end. On a single par-3, BOTH teammates (a1 AND a2) are checked and neither opponent is => the team wins TWO greenies (+2) on one hole. No carry involved.
_contract: Golden HAND-CALC fixture for the F1 greenie modifier (Story 2.2). COUNT model: rawA = (#teamA boxes) - (#teamB boxes); both A teammates inside both opponents => rawA=+2 => the team scores TWO greenie team points on this hole. Software just counts the boxes; at most one team legitimately scores (closest-to-pin is a human judgment). All nets = par => base points 0; ledger is greenie-only. SettlementEdge IR is the post-Story-2.1a whole-dollar 1-to-1 layout (an even point count yields whole-dollar legs directly). Integer cents; fromPlayerId PAYS toPlayerId.
_handCalc:
Teams A={a1,a2} B={b1,b2}, flat $5/point, greenie ON. Single hole 3 (par 3), all nets = par => base points 0. GREENIE: a1 box + a2 box, b none => countA=2 countB=0 => rawA=+2, WON (no carry). award = +2 to A. pointsByHole={3:+2}, finalCarry=0, settleablePar3Count=1.

SETTLE: pts(H3)=+2. Each cross pair += 2*250=500 => each A +1000c (+$10), each B -1000c. EDGES (1-to-1, +2 is even => whole-dollar directly): b1->a1 1000, b2->a2 1000. ledgerTotal=2000c.

CONSERVATION (carryover ON): Sum|pts| + finalCarry = 2 + 0 = 2 = RHS over settleable par-3s (H3 |rawA|=2). Agrees.
